results.condition_check: No matching DPIA-triggering condition or assistance context is stated; the clause is about breach notification content.
results.modality_check: The sub-item is descriptive content under a mandatory parent, but it still concerns a different subject matter.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object, recipient, and purpose: breach mitigation/notification versus DPIA assistance.
results.contract_source_text: reasonable measures taken or proposed by the Processor to mitigate the consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for a sub-processor's performance, while the contract only states that Microsoft is a sub-processor providing hosting services and where data are hosted; it does not allocate liability.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition in the contract; the liability requirement is unconditional and unaddressed.
results.modality_check: The contract is descriptive only and contains no obligation of liability or equivalent restrictive commitment.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location constraint can substitute for the missing liability undertaking; the hosting-location statements are unrelated to liability.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor has entered into an agreement with Microsoft Corporation whereby Microsoft is a Sub-processor to the Processor acting (on the Controller's behalf) to provide hosting services via the Microsoft Azure Cloud Platform. All of Controller's data including Personal Data are hosted on the Microsoft Azure Cloud Platform in Ireland and the Netherlands. The hosting services do not take place in countries outside EU.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the DPA to state the categories of data subjects (classes of persons). The contract parent/sub-item states categories of data, and the sub-item names 'Sensitive data (cf. GDPR article 9)', which is a type of personal data, not a category of data subjects. Wrong axis.
results.condition_check: No specific condition issue; the mismatch is substantive/axis-based.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is permissive ('may fall within'), but modality is not the main issue here because the required content is on the wrong axis.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location issue; the clause is a content pointer to data categories, not data-subject categories.
results.contract_source_text: Sensitive data (cf. GDPR article 9)*.

results.core_alignment_check: Processor action concerns implementing agreed instruction changes; regulation requires processor to inform controller of a legal requirement before processing. Different obligation, different object/recipient.
results.condition_check: No matching condition about legal requirement/public interest prohibition.
results.modality_check: Mandatory language, but on a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs and is attached to implementation of changes, not pre-processing notice.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall without undue delay initiate implementation of agreed changes of the instructions and shall ensure that such changes are implemented without undue delay in relation to the nature and extent of the changes.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the DPA to state the categories of data subjects. The parent clause introduces an in-line list of categories of Data Subjects, and the sub-item 'Citizens of Controller' is one such category of persons, matching the required axis.
results.condition_check: No special condition in the requirement; the clause is unconditional.
results.modality_check: Mandatory in context. The parent clause uses 'will' and introduces the categories as part of the DPA content, so the sub-item functions as a required listed category rather than a mere permission.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting timing, manner, or location constraints. The category is stated in-line, not deferred elsewhere.
results.contract_source_text: Citizens of Controller

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires stating the types of personal data, and the contract names categories of data that may be processed, which is the same content axis.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern the personal data processed under the DPA, with no conflicting condition.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'may fall within' is permissive wording for the listed data categories, but the clause still states the categories; the regulation requires the DPA to contain them, not a stronger duty on the processor.
results.other_constraints_check: No additional timing, manner, target, or location constraints are at issue.
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data Processed may fall within any of the following categories of data.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation prohibits processor transfers of personal data to a third country or international organization unless authorized by the controller; the contract requires processing only in accordance with the controller's instructions, expressly including transfers to a third country or international organisation, which is the same real-world transfer-authorisation obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation requires prior specific or general authorization by the controller; the contract allows such transfers only in accordance with the controller's instructions, which is at least as strict and covers authorization/instruction before transfer. The legal-compulsion exception is an additional permitted circumstance and does not weaken the core rule.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation is prohibitive/mandatory, and the contract is equally restrictive by limiting transfers to controller instructions and otherwise forbidding them except where required by applicable law.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The target is the same: transfers of personal data to a third country or international organisation. The contract is not looser on timing or scope; it is at least as strict.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to contractually impose the Article 28(3) GDPR obligations on engaged sub-processors. The contract clause instead governs prior notice and objection rights for changes to sub-processors under general written authorisation; it does not itself impose the Article 28(3) obligations on sub-processors.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered whenever the processor engages sub-processors; the contract clause is limited to intended changes/addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorisation and controller objection timing.
results.modality_check: The regulation requires a mandatory contractual imposition on sub-processors. The contract clause is mandatory only as to notification and objection mechanics, not as to passing through the required obligations.
results.other_constraints_check: The relevant constraint is by contract or other legal act imposing the same GDPR obligations on sub-processors. The clause addresses a different mechanism (notice/consent/acceptance) and omits the substantive pass-through obligation.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Processor/sub-processor authorization and objection procedure vs processor imposing Article 28(3) obligations on sub-processors by contract or legal act: different obligation; the clause regulates notice/consent for changing sub-processors, not flow-down of GDPR terms.
results.condition_check: The regulation applies to engaged sub-processors generally; the contract clause applies only in the case of general written authorisation and intended changes/addition/replacement of sub-processors.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory notice/objection mechanics, but it does not impose the required contractual/legal-act obligations on sub-processors.
results.other_constraints_check: Different target and substance: authorization/objection timing for sub-processor changes, not the substantive Article 28(3) obligations to be imposed on sub-processors.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in consulting supervisory authorities prior to processing in high-risk cases, while the contract only requires the processor to implement technical and organisational measures to ensure GDPR compliance and protect data subjects; it does not address consultation or assistance to the controller.
results.condition_check: No matching condition: the regulation is triggered by high-risk processing absent mitigating measures and prior consultation, which is not addressed.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory obligation ('agrees to implement'), but on a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/location/target overlap with prior consultation of supervisory authorities.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: processor action on instructions: the contract requires the processor to implement agreed changes to the controller's instructions, which presupposes processing in accordance with documented instructions; same general obligation but narrower than the regulation's baseline instruction-compliance duty
results.condition_check: compatible: both concern instructions from the controller; contract adds the qualifier that changes must be agreed, which is not weaker for the covered situation
results.modality_check: mandatory: 'shall' imposes an obligation
results.other_constraints_check: compatible and at least as strict on timing/manner: 'without undue delay' is not looser than the regulation's documented-instructions requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall without undue delay initiate implementation of agreed changes of the instructions and shall ensure that such changes are implemented without undue delay in relation to the nature and extent of the changes.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the types of personal data, and the contract text expressly mentions 'types of Personal Data' as part of the processing details.
results.condition_check: No special condition in either text; compatible.
results.modality_check: The regulation is mandatory and the contract states the types are specified as part of the agreement, which is at least as strong.
results.other_constraints_check: The only issue is locus: the contract points to Appendix 1 for the details rather than stating the types in the clause itself, but Appendix 1 is part of the Agreement and the clause identifies that the types are included there.
results.contract_source_text: The details of the Processing of Personal Data, and in particular the categories of Data Subjects, types of Personal Data and the purposes for which they are Processed, are specified in Appendix 1, which forms an integral part of the Agreement. 3. Security measures

results.core_alignment_check: No substantive overlap: the regulation requires a controller right to object to intended sub-processor changes after notice, while the contract text is only a fragment referring to a general or specific authorisation in Appendix 5 and does not itself state notice, objection rights, or change control.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered by intended changes to addition/replacement of sub-processors after the controller is informed; the contract fragment does not address that condition.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is a processor prohibition absent controller approval, but the sub-item fragment is incomplete and does not impose the controller's right to object.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, or recipient details matching the notice-and-objection mechanism are stated; the text is also a pointer to Appendix 5 rather than the substantive rule.
results.contract_source_text: a general or specific authorisation according to Appendix 5 to this Agreement or

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller immediately when an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection law. The contract instead requires persons working for the processor with access to personal data to process it only as instructed by the controller, subject to legal requirements. That is a different obligation (processing limitation), not a notification duty.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / different trigger. The regulation is triggered by an instruction that infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions; the contract is triggered by any processing by persons with access to personal data and an exception for processing required by law.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language ('must'), but it imposes a different duty rather than the required notification obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires immediacy and informing the controller; the contract contains no notice/reporting timing or communication duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor must ensure that the persons performing work for the Processor and who have access to Personal Data, only process such Personal Data as instructed by the Controller, unless processing is required under applicable EU law or national legislation. 13. Liability

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of any intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors under a general written authorization. The contract text only states that Microsoft is currently a sub-processor providing hosting services and where data are hosted; it does not impose any duty to notify the controller of intended changes or additions/replacements.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered in case of general written authorization and intended changes to sub-processors. The contract does not address that trigger or any notification condition.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory duty to inform. The contract contains no comparable obligation, only descriptive statements about current arrangements.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, or recipient-equivalent notification obligation is present; the clause is about current hosting and location, not notice of changes.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor has entered into an agreement with Microsoft Corporation whereby Microsoft is a Sub-processor to the Processor acting (on the Controller's behalf) to provide hosting services via the Microsoft Azure Cloud Platform. All of Controller's data including Personal Data are hosted on the Microsoft Azure Cloud Platform in Ireland and the Netherlands. The hosting services do not take place in countries outside EU.

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires processor assistance to the controller in responding to data subject rights requests, while the contract clause is a general security/compliance commitment to implement technical and organisational measures and protect data subjects' rights; it does not specifically require assistance with handling rights requests.
results.condition_check: Compatible in the abstract, but the triggering situation is different: the regulation is about requests for exercising data subject rights.
results.modality_check: The contract uses an affirmative obligation ('agrees to implement'), which is not weaker than 'shall', but it is directed to a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing/manner/target constraint for assisting the controller with rights requests; the clause instead concerns general GDPR-compliant processing and protection of rights.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both address the processor's use of sub-processors under controller authorization. The contract covers the general written authorization route and requires prior notice plus an opportunity to object, with deemed acceptance absent objection; this is the same real-world obligation as requiring prior specific or general written authorization before engaging a sub-processor.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation applies to engaging a sub-processor without prior specific or general written authorization; the contract specifically addresses the general written authorization scenario and the controller's objection mechanism, which is the same condition set for that route.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger. The regulation prohibits engagement absent prior authorization. The contract is restrictive and requires notice/opportunity to object, with acceptance only if the controller does not object within 30 days, which is not weaker than the regulatory requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The timing and target are substantively aligned: prior notice before changes, controller objection opportunity, and deemed acceptance after 30 days are consistent with a prior authorization framework.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require implementing appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk for personal data processing; the contract expressly mirrors the Art. 32(1) obligation and applies it to the processor (and controller jointly), with added examples of measures.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on taking into account state of the art, implementation costs, nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, and the risk to rights and freedoms of natural persons.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall implement', which is at least as strong as the regulation's mandatory 'shall implement'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract includes the same security objective and is at least as specific/strict by listing example measures such as encryption and ensuring confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the clause only points to where security-measure details are located (Appendix 2) and does not itself state the measures; it is a pointer rather than the substantive obligation/content required by Art. 32(1).
results.condition_check: No substantive condition is stated in the contract clause.
results.modality_check: No operative modality for the Art. 32(1) measures is stated; the clause is descriptive/pointer language.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, or location constraints for the required security measures are actually delivered here beyond the cross-reference to Appendix 2.
results.contract_source_text: The details of the security measures taken by Processor in this respect of the Processing of Personal Data, are specified in Appendix 2, which forms an integral part of the Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires one possible security measure: restoring availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident. The contract sub-item states exactly that ability, with the same object (Personal Data), same trigger (physical or technical incident), and same timing standard (timely manner).
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on a physical or technical incident.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract is an included security measure within a mandatory clause requiring the controller and processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures; the sub-item is part of that mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing and target are substantively the same; the contract wording is not weaker than the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to immediately inform the controller when an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection law. The contract instead addresses using reasonable endeavours to comply with legislative changes, not notifying the controller of unlawful instructions. It is about change-management/termination, not the same reporting obligation.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered by an instruction that infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions. The contract is triggered by failure to agree on reasonable terms for implementing changes deemed necessary to comply with GDPR or other laws. These are different situations.
results.modality_check: The contract contains obligations and permissions around compliance efforts and termination rights, but no obligation to inform the controller immediately of an infringing instruction.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing requirement of immediate notice is absent; the contract instead provides a 60-day written notice termination mechanism, which is a different and much looser mechanism.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to immediately inform the controller when an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions. The contract clause instead addresses legislative changes, implementing changes to instructions, and termination rights if the parties cannot agree on terms; it does not require the processor to notify the controller of an unlawful instruction.
results.condition_check: The triggering condition is different: the regulation is triggered by an instruction that infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions, while the contract is triggered by legislative changes and failure to agree on terms for implementing changes.
results.modality_check: The contract does not impose an equivalent obligation to inform; it mainly provides that the processor is not obligated to implement changes and that either party may terminate.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires immediate notice to the controller; the contract contains no comparable notice-to-controller duty or equivalent timing.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract requires the processor to notify the controller when a supervisory authority contacts the processor; the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority. results.other_constraints_check: Different target and direction of communication; the contract notifies the controller, while the regulation concerns assisting notification to the supervisory authority.
results.contract_source_text: To notify Controller in the event that a supervisory authority contacts Processor, insofar as permitted by law. 6. Personal Data breach

results.core_alignment_check: Processor security assessment obligation matches the regulation's requirement to take into account risks to personal data when determining security level; same actor (processor), same action (assess/take into account), same object (security level/risk to personal data), same recipient not applicable.

results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses mandatory 'shall' / 'account shall be taken', which is at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the listed risks and data states (transmitted, stored or otherwise processed) substantially match the regulation's timing/scope and are not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring security of processing. results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.modality_check: The regulation is a liability rule ('shall be liable only where...'). The contract is about payment entitlement and does not impose or mirror that liability standard.

results.contract_source_text: In the event Controller requires assistance of Processor or the Processor’s Sub-processors pursuant to this Agreement (including, but not limited to clauses 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 5.6, 5.7 and/or clause 7.2), this assistance is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3, which forms an integral part of the Agreement. Notwithstanding the before mentioned, Processor is not entitled to remuneration for circumstances which are attributable to Processor’s breach of security or Processor’s breach of it’s obligations as set out in this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for a sub-processor's obligations; the contract instead limits the processor's liability to direct damages with a cap, which is a different and narrower liability allocation.

results.core_alignment_check: processor transfer of personal data to a third party provider/integration service is the same real-world obligation as transferring personal data to a third country or international organization only with controller authorization; the clause gives prior authorization from the controller, though limited to a specific use case

results.other_constraints_check: compatible in substance: the contract is narrower because it applies only when the controller uses specified third-party integration services and only to the extent necessary, but that is stricter rather than looser than the regulation's authorization condition
results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: No substantive overlap: the regulation imposes liability on any controller involved in processing for GDPR-infringing processing damage, while the contract text only appears to be a timing/exception fragment within a processor-liability limitation and does not state controller liability for GDPR damage.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures for processing. results.core_alignment_check: Yes. results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on taking into account the state of the art, implementation costs, nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, and risk to rights and freedoms; the contract is substantively aligned and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses mandatory 'shall implement' language, which meets the regulation's obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract includes equivalent security measures and the same security objective; the encryption scope is at least comparable (and narrower in a stricter way), and the confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience requirement is directly covered.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to take into account security risks, specifically accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to personal data when assessing security. The contract parent clause requires appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk, including the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services. The sub-item adds the ability to restore availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident. This is a security measure addressing availability/resilience, which is substantively within the same security-risk assessment obligation, though narrower than the full list.
results.condition_check: Both apply in the context of assessing/ensuring security of processing and risk to personal data; the contract's condition of a physical or technical incident is compatible with the regulation's broader risk-based security assessment.

It does not contradict the required security factors, but it only covers one aspect of them.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

This is the same real-world obligation and is at least as specific/strict.

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation permits the processor to process personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law. The contract instead says the processor will use reasonable endeavours to comply with legislative changes and addresses failure to agree on changes to instructions, with termination rights. It does not clearly authorize processing without instructions under a legal requirement, nor does it impose the same processor right/duty.
results.condition_check: The regulation's trigger is processing required by Union or Member State law. The contract's trigger is inability to agree on reasonable terms for implementing changes to instructions, with a reference to changes deemed necessary for GDPR or other applicable EU/national data protection laws. This is related but not the same condition and is framed around amendment/termination rather than processing without instructions.
results.modality_check: The regulation is a permission/exception allowing processing without documented instructions. The contract imposes a reasonable-endeavours obligation and termination mechanics, which are not equivalent to the specific permission.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract concerns legislative changes, implementation of instruction changes, and termination on disagreement; it does not address the specific location/manner constraint of processing without documented instructions under legal compulsion.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires that, under a general written authorization, the controller be informed of intended sub-processor changes and have a right to object to additions or replacements. The contract clause only requires the processor to impose data protection obligations on sub-processors and ensure sufficient guarantees; it does not grant controller notice or objection rights.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered by intended changes to the addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization. The contract clause is triggered by engaging sub-processors for processing activities and refers to Appendix 5, but not to controller notification or objection.
results.modality_check: The regulation requires a controller right to object. The contract clause imposes obligations on the processor/sub-processor but contains no corresponding controller right, so the modality is not matched.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation specifically concerns changes to sub-processor addition or replacement after prior information to the controller. The contract clause concerns downstream contractual safeguards for sub-processors and appropriate technical and organisational measures, which is a different duty.
results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation gives the controller a right to terminate the DPA in certain cases, while the contract clause concerns prior notice and objection/acceptance of changes to sub-processors, not termination of the DPA.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the contract conditions are about intended sub-processor changes and a 30-day objection window, not cases triggering a right to terminate the DPA.
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory about notification and deemed acceptance, but it does not grant a termination right at all.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires substantive security measures (e.g. pseudonymisation/encryption, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, timely restoration, regular testing/evaluation), while the contract clause only states that measures and security level in Appendix 2 are sufficient to comply with clause 3 and points to an appendix rather than itself setting out the measures.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring security of processing. The contract parent/sub-item mainly sets out security measures, risk-based assessment, hosting location, and a cost schedule for assistance/audits, but it does not itself impose an assistance obligation to help the controller ensure security; it only mentions assistance being charged if requested.
results.condition_check: The regulation is a general assistance duty for ensuring security; the contract text conditions assistance on the controller requiring it and frames it as a chargeable item, which is not the same substantive obligation.
results.modality_check: The contract language is not an affirmative processor duty to assist in this respect; it is descriptive and cost-related, with no clear mandatory assistance commitment matching the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: The security-related content concerns technical and organisational measures and hosting/security arrangements, not the specific assistance-to-controller obligation required by the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.core_alignment_check: Processor assistance with notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority is not addressed; the clause is about controller access to delete/return/export data and processor deletion on expiry.
results.condition_check: No matching breach-notification condition is present.
results.modality_check: No assistance obligation regarding breach notification is present.
results.other_constraints_check: The clause concerns data deletion/return/export and retention on expiry, not breach notification timing, target, or authority.
results.contract_source_text: Processor will give Controller access to system functionality in order for the Controller to delete and/or return (i.e. export) any and all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data during the Agreement. Upon expiry of the Agreement the Processor will delete all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data, unless EU or national Member State law requires storage of the Personal Data.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller with notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority. The contract clause is about giving the controller access to delete/return/export data and deleting data on expiry; it does not address breach notification assistance.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching breach-notification condition is present.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for deletion/export access, but that obligation is unrelated to the required breach-notification assistance.
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive overlap on subject, timing, recipient, or action. The clause concerns data deletion/return on request or expiry, not breach notification to a supervisory authority.
results.contract_source_text: Processor will give Controller access to system functionality in order for the Controller to delete and/or return (i.e. export) any and all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data during the Agreement. Upon expiry of the Agreement the Processor will delete all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data, unless EU or national Member State law requires storage of the Personal Data.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; the contract clause is about legislative changes, implementing changes to instructions, and termination rights, not about restricting personnel processing to controller instructions.
results.condition_check: No matching condition: the regulation applies whenever a natural person under the processor's authority has access to personal data; the contract instead conditions obligations on legislative changes and agreement on reasonable terms.
results.modality_check: The contract uses obligations about reasonable endeavours and termination, but it does not impose the required instruction-only processing duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive overlap on manner/timing/target/location/cause; the clause concerns change-management and termination, not processing under authority or instruction compliance.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational security measures; the contract expressly provides for 'a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing.'
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern the ongoing/regular evaluation of security measures; the contract does not narrow the trigger and is at least as broad.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract language is mandatory within the agreement context and is not merely permissive.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the subject matter, security objective, and scope of measures/processing are aligned; no contradictory timing or location constraint weakens the duty.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires implementing security measures (pseudonymisation/encryption, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, timely restoration, and regular testing/evaluation). The contract clause only defines what a 'Personal Data Breach' is; it does not impose any security measure or obligation.
results.condition_check: Not applicable; the clause is a definition and does not set comparable triggering conditions for the required security measures.
results.modality_check: Not applicable; the clause is definitional, not an obligation with comparable force.
results.other_constraints_check: Not applicable; no timing, manner, target, or location constraints for the required measures are provided.
results.contract_source_text: ‘Personal Data Breach’ shall mean a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise Processed;

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the DPA to state the duration of processing, while the contract text only names a category of data subjects ('Controller’s employees, board members and officers') under a lead-in about categories of data subjects.
results.condition_check: No specific condition in the requirement; the contract text is not a duration statement.
results.modality_check: Not applicable to the required content; the clause is descriptive and does not provide the required duration.
results.other_constraints_check: Axis mismatch: categories of data subjects are not the duration of processing, and the parent clause points to a list of data-subject categories rather than a processing period.
results.contract_source_text: Controller’s employees, board members and officers

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA to state at least one processor's identity and contact details. The contract text instead assigns responsibility to the Controller for lawful processing basis and then has a heading 'Data Subjects'; it does not provide any processor identity or contact details.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition in the regulation; the contract does not address the required content.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for a different duty, but not for providing processor identity/contact details.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching content requirement is stated; the 'Data Subjects' heading is not the processor identity/contact details required, and there is no in-line enumeration of those details.
results.contract_source_text: It is the sole responsibility and liability of the Controller to ensure the necessary basis of lawful Processing for the transfer of the Controller’s Personal Data to and from any third party provider of an integration service and/or application that is used by the Controller via Processor.com and the Processing by any third party provider of the Controller’s Personal Data in this respect. 2. Data Subjects

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the DPA to state the categories of data subjects, but the contract clause concerns deletion process/timeframes for the controller’s data and personal data, not data-subject categories.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition in either text.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a descriptive statement about deletion procedures, not a weaker permission; modality is not the issue.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching content on categories of data subjects; the appendix reference is a pointer to deletion details, not the required content.
results.contract_source_text: The process and timeframes for deletion of Controller’s data including the Personal Data is described in Appendix 4, which forms an integral part of the Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA to state the duration of the processing. The contract text only lists a type of processing service/activity ('Provision of various online data processing Services...') and does not state any processing duration.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition is stated for duration; the parent clause only introduces a list of possible activities depending on scope and nature of the main agreement.
results.modality_check: The contract text is descriptive, not an obligation to contain the duration.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/duration information is provided; the clause is a topic list item and not the required content.
results.contract_source_text: Provision of various online data processing Services including among others a survey and reporting tool via software solutions and platform made available from Processor.com.
